title: Due Scheduling, Monotonic Clocks, Backoff, Jitter, and Pause Policy · Execution Trace: Feed Go Success Adaptation
summary: A ticker polls every feed at one interval, wall-clock jumps duplicate work, failures retry in lockstep, successful publishers never adapt, and paused sources remain due. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go success adaptation increment in deterministic adaptive polling scheduler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Adjust cadence from update history and publisher guidance without exceeding freshness or publisher budgets.
Apply bounded exponential backoff with deterministic-testable jitter, reset rules, Retry-After, and maximum staleness escalation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Success Adaptation · Theory Feed Go Success Adaptation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5626 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove persisted due state, injected clocks, adaptive backoff, jitter, pause, and non-overlap; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
